title: Shopping Safely Online
drDigitalIntro: Online shopping is convenient, but some sites are fake. Before buying: check that the address bar shows 'https://' with a padlock icon (means it's encrypted), look for reviews and a contact address, avoid deals that seem too good to be true, and use a credit card (not debit) for better fraud protection. Well-known retailers are generally safe. For a new store, search its name plus 'reviews' or 'scam' first.

playgroundTask.goal: You learned to identify safe online shopping links

steps.say: Go to the Phishing tab to inspect some shopping links.
That's shop.example.com with https — mark it Safe.
That URL is suspicious — mark it Dangerous.

drDigitalSuccess: You know how to spot safe vs. dangerous shopping links! HTTPS + a known domain = safe to buy.
drDigitalHint: Click Inspect to see a link's real URL, then decide if it's from a trustworthy site.
